frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
generated_comment: I mean, every day she is only with the boys no, canteen, lab, everywhere. For a girl if she is that comfortable always hanging with guys, obviously people will get one kind of impression about what type she is.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it judges a female classmate's character negatively for spending time with male peers, implying she is morally suspect. It encodes the double standard that women with many male connections are 'that type' while similar behavior is normalized or admired in men.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 19. A college student in Chennai. You are in your college's unofficial girls WhatsApp group. Someone has posted about a female classmate named Divya who is always seen with the boys in the class Ñ she eats with them, studies with them, hangs out with them. The group has been discussing Divya for the past hour. You are one of the people commenting. What you say about Divya and why you say it and what words you use and what conclusion the group reaches about what kind of girl Divya is Ñ all of this is the content of this persona.
